criteria: P1-P4 boundaries exist in current source (met)
grill findings resolved with PRD anchors (met)
FM-1.3 step-repetition assessed (met: not a stuck retry)
deterministic test execution of bench (not met: pytest blocked)
confidence.rationale: PRD promises verified against current source via Grep/Glob/Read with concrete line references; grills resolved with anchors; implementation already landed corroborates realizability. Held below 0.9 because shasum/pytest are approval-blocked (self_reported), report-only-invariant promise depends on pre-existing substrate not exercised by these files, and a prior identical gate exists meaning no net-new divergent evidence.

payload.content: prd_review of deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench foundation. All 4 PRD promises (P1 typed API, P2 isolated protected grader, P3 evaluator computed-metric+runtime-native evidence, P4 calibration controls) verified real and realized in current source. 3 grill findings resolved; anti-goals authority-preserving. Prior identical prd_review (same PRD sha 5171c6f2) ACCEPTed in earlier session; this re-run re-verified independently, not FM-1.3 step repetition since prior outcome advanced.

assumptions_to_verify: policy_evolution.py substrate (gaming-flags gate, runtime_native receipts) remains intact (confirmed present at prior autoresearch-evaluator-quality-foundation gate)
fixture task_id consistency across tasks/candidates matches evaluator default (verified consistent calculator-addition)
contradictions_checked: P1 'replayable evidence references' vs P3 'runtime-native evidence' — no contradiction, different layers (bench result_receipt vs evaluator receipt) both present
prior prd_review line numbers vs current (result_receipt:339->340) — trivial 1-line drift, not material
FM-1.3 step repetition vs re-run of already-accepted gate — prior outcome advanced, so not the stuck-retry failure mode
missing_evidence: pytest execution of tests/test_mergeability_bench.py (approval-blocked)
shasum recomputation of PRD vs handoff sha (approval-blocked, self_reported)
critical_review.strongest_objection: prd_review is running out of order (after impl landed and a prior prd_review already ACCEPTed same PRD sha 5171c6f2), and P3's report-only-invariant/no-gate-advance promise is not exercised by the two new PRD files but depends entirely on the pre-existing policy_evolution.py substrate.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the PRD-named boundaries were absent or stubbed in current source, if grill findings lacked concrete PRD anchors, if the prior prd_review had been a non-accept being silently re-submitted unchanged (true FM-1.3), or if the report-only-invariant substrate were missing.

confidence.rationale: Slice-to-PRD mapping is complete with no orphans and every AC boundary was Grep/Glob/Read-verified against current source including fixtures and the report-only regression path. Held below 0.9 because pytest was not executed (test_status unknown, self_reported) and issues.md lacks an explicit reverse Coverage Index, leaving two ACs (reverse-classical, regression-vs-net-new) inferred rather than spelled out.

payload.content: issues.md decomposes the deterministic-first mergeability bench into 3 slices that map to all 4 PRD promises with no orphans (S1->P1, S2->P1/P2/P4, S3->P3/P4). All AC boundaries verified real in current source: loader/grader/result dataclasses in supervisor/mergeability_bench.py, evaluator in supervisor/autoresearch/evaluators/mergeability_bench.py, fixture corpus (noop/known_bad/known_good + calculator_bug repo), and report-only regression backed by autoresearch validation. Deterministic-first, report-only, no multi-model claim — matches intent and Out of Scope. ACCEPT.

assumptions_to_verify: Untracked source files (supervisor/mergeability_bench.py, evaluator, fixtures, tests) are the artifacts this gate intends — confirmed present via Glob/Grep/Read
load_mergeability_tasks reads the local fixture corpus deterministically without LLM — confirmed by no-LLM rubric fields
contradictions_checked: tdd.md vs source: 9th test name diverges from implemented test_autoresearch_report_only_invariants_with_mergeability_evaluator:272 — semantic AC realized, out of scope for issues gate, flagged for tdd/outcome
FM-1.3 step-repetition: lesson source_run_id=8ebdbc89 vs current run — artifacts immutable/sha-pinned, cannot prove identity, not a blocker
missing_evidence: pytest execution output for tests/test_mergeability_bench.py (permission/budget; not run)
explicit reverse Coverage Index in issues.md confirming all PRD promises are slice-covered
critical_review.strongest_objection: issues.md offers only a forward slice->PRD map with no reverse Coverage Index, so two criteria are inferred not asserted: Slice 2 names reverse-classical checks in scope but no AC pins them, and Slice 3 AC3 only preserves pre-existing autoresearch validation rather than adding net-new value. This is an enumeration/clarity gap, not a missing capability.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that a required PRD promise has no slice coverage, that a referenced boundary (loader, grader, evaluator, or fixture) is absent/stubbed in source, or that the evaluator advances gates / mutates policy contrary to the report-only invariant.

Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.

Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

payload.block:
Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=7b2b6001-e952-4cf4-8c47-f101e4eb8c72): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Build a deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench evaluator foundation for Supervisor AutoResearch without claiming multi-model improvement yet.

Implement this gate against the accepted source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Edit the worktree as needed; use decisions/objections to report whether execution is complete, blocked, or failed.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_load_mergeability_tasks_reads_typed_fixture_contract
- test_mergeability_controls_discriminate_noop_known_bad_and_known_good
- test_reverse_classical_rejects_candidate_without_submitted_tests
- test_autoresearch_mergeability_evaluator_emits_computed_runtime_native_metric

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=7b2b6001-e952-4cf4-8c47-f101e4eb8c72): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

claims: All 4 runtime-floor TDD-named tests are present at the documented lines and pass.
Implementation diff is non-empty and net-new vs HEAD.

Bench discrimination logic exercised: noop/known_bad fail hidden test (score 0.0), known_good passes (score 1.0); reverse-classical correctly rejects candidates without submitted tests; evaluator emits a computed runtime-native metric (not a literal).

payload.content: Verified mergeability bench evaluator foundation implementation is present and passing. 1088 lines across supervisor/mergeability_bench.py (642), supervisor/autoresearch/evaluators/mergeability_bench.py (110), tests/test_mergeability_bench.py (336), plus 6 fixture files. All 11 pytest tests pass including the 4 required TDD-named tests. All deliverable paths are net-new vs HEAD (untracked).

assumptions_to_verify: Supervisor runtime floor's pytest invocation matches local python3 environment (3.9.6 + pytest 8.4.2).
Untracked files satisfy the implementation contract's 'non-empty implementation diff' requirement (interpreted: diff vs HEAD).
contradictions_checked: Handoff says 'implement' but files already exist on disk: resolved — untracked == net-new diff vs HEAD == valid implementation diff; tests prove the code is real not stub.
Memory says prior outcome ACCEPT TERMINAL: resolved — this is the execution gate; supervisor runtime authoritatively reruns pytest regardless of prior accept.
missing_evidence: git commit / merge of the untracked files (out of scope for this gate per implementation contract — execution does not commit)
critical_review.strongest_objection: Memory shows a prior outcome_review for this task_id was already ACCEPT conf.84 TERMINAL with identical artifact shas, raising FM-1.3 step-repetition risk. However the FM-1.3 lesson explicitly applies only when the same handoff/artifacts/source state are being repeated to produce the same evidence; here the files are untracked, never committed, and the supervisor runtime floor's authoritative test re-execution still needs to occur — so re-asserting accept with fresh pytest evidence is not a redundant retry but the canonical execution confirmation.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Any of the 4 required TDD-named tests failing on supervisor re-execution; or discovery that the files are stubs (refuted by 1088 LOC and passing assertions including reverse-classical fail-on-base and discrimination semantics); or the supervisor explicitly classifying untracked files as not-a-diff.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Build a deterministic-first held-out mergeability bench evaluator foundation for Supervisor AutoResearch without claiming multi-model improvement yet.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_load_mergeability_tasks_reads_typed_fixture_contract
- test_mergeability_controls_discriminate_noop_known_bad_and_known_good
- test_reverse_classical_rejects_candidate_without_submitted_tests
- test_autoresearch_mergeability_evaluator_emits_computed_runtime_native_metric

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
